title: CLAUDE.md
text: radiatR R package plotting analyzing animal movement trajectories circular/radial arenas. handles full pipeline: camera calibration → data import → coordinate transformation → circular statistics → visualization.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: commands run R (Rscript). project uses renv dependency isolation — .Rprofile activates automatically.
code: # Install/restore dependencies renv::restore()  # Rebuild documentation from Roxygen2 tags (run before check) devtools::document()  # Run all tests devtools::test()  # Run a single test file testthat::test_file("tests/testthat/test-circular_statistics.R")  # Full R CMD CHECK devtools::check()  # Install locally devtools::install_local(".")

title: CLAUDE.md
text: R/TrajSet.R defines central container. TrajSet holds list trajectory tibbles plus metadata (arena geometry, transform history). Almost every downstream function either accepts returns TrajSet. Understand class touching module.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Calibration (R/calibration.R, R/calibration_capture.R) — maps pixel coordinates real-world positions via CalModel S4 object. cam_cal_pt / cam_cal_many build models; calibrate_positions (TrajSet method) applies . Import (R/loaders.R) — largest module. Reads manifest files describe track data lives, dispatches format-specific readers (TrajSet_read_*). loader format registry (register_loader_*) lets callers add new file formats without modifying core code. Coordinate transformation (R/circular_trials.R, R/circular_mapping.R) — converts Cartesian pixel coordinates unit-circle angles. circular_trials.R extracts per-trial segments normalises unit arena; circular_mapping.R handles pixel→angle arithmetic. Heading computation (R/headings.R) — derives directional headings position sequences. Uses rule registry pattern (analogous loader registry) custom heading rules can registered applied name. Statistics (R/circular_statistics.R) — thin wrapper around circular package. circ_summary main entry point. Visualisation (R/circular_plotting.R) — radiate() primary plot function; returns ggplot2 object. Helper functions (add_ticks, add_circ, degree_labs, etc.) return ggplot2 layers designed added +.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: New file formats: use register_loader_read_function / register_loader_info_function (see R/loaders.R). New heading rules: use register_heading_rule (see R/headings.R). registries follow pattern: named list stored package env, dispatched runtime.

title: Camera Calibration Workflow
text: calibration helpers expect three key ingredients: Intrinsic matrix (K) – 3 × 3 matrix usual form [fx0cx0fycy001]\left[\begin{smallmatrix}f_x & 0 & c_x \ 0 & f_y & c_y \ 0 & 0 & 1\end{smallmatrix}\right] c_x / c_y principal point pixels f_x / f_y focal lengths, also pixels. Distortion coefficients (k) – passed either numeric vector, named vector (e.g., c(k1 = ..., k2 = ..., p1 = ..., p2 = ...)), list radial / tangential entries. Missing coefficients assumed zero. Metric focal length (F) – physical focal length (scaling factor) millimetres. Supply scalar isotropic scaling length-2 vector differentiate axes. can bundle CalModel object repeated use trajectory sets.

title: Camera Calibration Workflow
text: calibrating checkerboard sequence often need world coordinates inner corners. helper checkerboard_points() mirrors MATLAB’s generateCheckerboardPoints can generate directly radiatR. Use square_size argument match physical square width (millimetres, centimetres, etc.) pass as_tibble = FALSE prefer simple matrix x/y coordinates.
code: cb_pts <- checkerboard_points(c(9, 7), square_size = 5) head(cb_pts) #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #>   corner   row   col     x     y #>    <int> <int> <int> <dbl> <dbl> #> 1      1     0     0     0     0 #> 2      2     1     0     0     5 #> 3      3     2     0     0    10 #> 4      4     3     0     0    15 #> 5      5     4     0     0    20 #> 6      6     5     0     0    25

title: Camera Calibration Workflow
text: Use cam_cal_pt() need correct single pixel coordinate, example transforming landmarks exported digitising tool. Internally function: Recentres point subtracting principal point (optically_center()), Normalises focal lengths (focalize()), Removes radial tangential distortion via fixed-point iteration (radial_distort()), Converts undistorted coordinates millimetres (scaled_xy2mm()). iteration count convergence tolerance configurable via max_iterations tolerance arguments.
code: raw_pt <- c(x = 650, y = 380) cam_cal_pt(raw_pt["x"], raw_pt["y"], K, distortion, F_mm) #> [1] 25.38876 14.87574

title: Camera Calibration Workflow
text: small calibration sets can grab corners manually. calibration_session() opens graphics device per frame records clicks row-major order, solves planar calibration: helper works chessboard, ChArUco, AprilTag grids—simply provide board_dims/square_size matching pattern. output contains CalModel, per-frame extrinsics, reprojection errors can inspect fit.
code: frames <- list('frame1.png', 'frame2.png') calib <- calibration_session(frames, pattern = 'chessboard', board_dims = c(9, 7), square_size = 5) calib$intrinsics

title: Camera Calibration Workflow
text: tables matrices points, cam_cal_many() processes everything one pass. Supplying CalModel lets calibrate entire trajectory sets single call. bundled P. lividus example data provides real pixel-space tracks recorded webcam mounted circular arena. load first trial apply hypothetical calibration model defined . real workflow, replace hypothetical K, distortion, F_mm values parameters estimated checkerboard calibration sequence captured camera lens.
code: pts <- matrix(   c(640, 360,     700, 420,     580, 320),   ncol = 2,   byrow = TRUE,   dimnames = list(NULL, c("x", "y")) ) cam_cal_many(pts, K, distortion, F_mm) #>          [,1]     [,2] #> [1,] 24.52197 13.82316 #> [2,] 27.91428 16.79090 #> [3,] 20.00529 11.05509 track_dir <- system.file("extdata", "tracks", package = "radiatR") manifest_path <- system.file("extdata", "P_lividus_trials.csv", package = "radiatR")  file_tbl <- import_tracks(track_dir) manifest <- import_info(manifest_path) file_tbl <- load_tracks(file_tbl, manifest, track_dir)  # Read the first track file in pixel space track_raw <- read.delim(   file.path(track_dir, file_tbl$track[1]),   sep = "\t", header = FALSE )[, 1:3] names(track_raw) <- c("frame", "x", "y") track_raw$id <- tools::file_path_sans_ext(basename(file_tbl$track[1]))  ts_px <- TrajSet(   track_raw,   id = "id",   time = "frame", x = "x", y = "y",   normalize_xy = FALSE )  ts_mm <- calibrate_positions(ts_px, cal_model) head(ts_mm@data) #>   frame        x        y                                id   radius     angle #> 1     1 41.44048 22.21084 WIN_20210201_11_24_19_Pro_point02 1185.073 0.4920075 #> 2     2 41.44048 22.21084 WIN_20210201_11_24_19_Pro_point02 1185.073 0.4920075 #> 3     3 41.71374 22.28286 WIN_20210201_11_24_19_Pro_point02 1189.399 0.4906202 #> 4     4 41.71374 22.28286 WIN_20210201_11_24_19_Pro_point02 1189.399 0.4906202 #> 5     5 41.86786 22.30621 WIN_20210201_11_24_19_Pro_point02 1191.870 0.4895237 #> 6     6 41.89993 22.31070 WIN_20210201_11_24_19_Pro_point02 1192.400 0.4892894

title: Camera Calibration Workflow
text: can stash clicked points later reuse. helpers write CSV rebuild calibration without reopening graphics device. calibrate_positions() updates x/y columns -place re-computes angle values reflect corrected metric geometry.
code: points_tbl <- calibration_points_tibble(calib$image_points) write_calibration_points(calib$image_points, "calibration-points.csv") replay <- calibration_from_points(board_dims = c(9, 7), square_size = 5,                                   image_points = points_tbl, quiet = TRUE) replay$intrinsics ts_px_cal <- calib$transform(ts_px) head(as.data.frame(ts_px_cal))

title: Camera Calibration Workflow
text: undistortion step uses fixed-point solver similar undistortPoints routine OpenCV. defaults (max_iterations = 5, tolerance = 1e-9) usually sufficient. distortion model extreme points far optical axis, can relax parameters: convergence fails, function returns last iterate; consider checking coefficients tightening stopping criteria.
code: cam_cal_pt(650, 380, K, distortion, F_mm, max_iterations = 10, tolerance = 1e-12)

title: Camera Calibration Workflow
text: Use cam_cal_pt() / cam_cal_many() one-points tables. Wrap parameters CalModel correct every trajectory TrajSet via calibrate_positions(). Distortion coefficients can radial-include tangential terms; unnamed coefficients fall back sensible ordering. helpers provide drop-path applying calibration results common toolboxes maintainers large tracking datasets.

title: Working with Loaders
text: radiatR provides flexible loader framework turns tracking exports wide variety systems TrajSet objects. helpers cover three typical tasks: Reading experiment manifests (import_info() import_tracks()). Ingesting trajectory tables TrajSet_read() TrajSet_read_dir(). Extending loader registry register_loader_dialect() custom formats. vignette walks step using bundled P. lividus example data finishes user-defined loader custom format.

title: Working with Loaders
text: package ships five baseline trials Paracentrotus lividus visual acuity experiment. Tracks stored paired tab-separated text files: _point01.txt holds two landmark rows (arena centre + reference point); _point02.txt holds full per-frame xy trajectory.

title: Working with Loaders
text: import_tracks() scans directory _point01.txt / _point02.txt pairs returns tibble basenames.

title: Working with Loaders
text: import_info() parses trial manifest CSV. Use cond_cols generate compound condition column multiple metadata fields.
code: manifest_path <- system.file("extdata", "P_lividus_trials.csv", package = "radiatR") manifest <- import_info(manifest_path, cond_cols = c("type", "arc")) manifest #>   trial type arc id stim_loc       date     time obstacle #> 1     1 Herm   0  4      -32 01/02/2021 11:24:00  marbles #> 2     2 Herm   0  6      148 04/02/2021 16:55:00  marbles #> 3     3 Herm   0  9       49 01/02/2021 11:32:00  marbles #> 4     4 Herm   0 32      266 04/02/2021 17:12:00  marbles #> 5     5 Herm   0 20       75 04/02/2021 17:30:00  marbles #>                                       file   cond #> 1 G1D_0_obstacle/WIN_20210201_11_24_19_Pro Herm_0 #> 2 G1D_0_obstacle/WIN_20210201_11_32_56_Pro Herm_0 #> 3 G1D_0_obstacle/WIN_20210204_16_55_13_Pro Herm_0 #> 4 G1D_0_obstacle/WIN_20210204_17_12_38_Pro Herm_0 #> 5 G1D_0_obstacle/WIN_20210204_17_30_19_Pro Herm_0

title: Working with Loaders
text: radiatR reads tabular (CSV/TSV) exports many tracking tools named dialects. dialect maps tool’s column conventions onto TrajSet model one position per individual per frame. Pass dialect name TrajSet_read(path, dialect = "name"); tool-specific options go dialect_args. table summarises currently handled. Two limitations general: Tabular text . Dialects read CSV/TSV (, optional packages installed, JSON/XML/Parquet). Native binary formats — NumPy .npz MATLAB .mat — read except noted (ctrax reads .mat via R.matlab package). Position, posture. radiatR models single position per individual per frame. Per-frame posture data (variable-length midline outline polylines) outside model. tool tracks discrete body keypoints, can loaded extra columns turned body-axis heading bodypart_axis rule pose_to_headings(); dense posture polylines ingested. tool’s export directly supported, two escape hatches remain: convert tidy CSV use TrajSet_read() explicit mapping, register custom dialect (see Registering Custom Dialect ).

Dialects live -memory current session. persist across projects, place registration call package .Rprofile source using radiatR.
code: fake_json <- tempfile(fileext = ".json") jsonlite::write_json(   list(     tracks = list(       A = list(timestamp = c(0, 1, 2), px = c(0, 1, 2), py = c(0, 0.2, 0.5)),       B = list(timestamp = c(0, 1, 2), px = c(1, 1.3, 1.5), py = c(0, -0.1, -0.2))     )   ),   fake_json,   auto_unbox = TRUE )  json_tracks_fn <- function(x) {   dat <- if (is.character(x) && file.exists(x)) jsonlite::fromJSON(x) else x   entries <- purrr::imap(dat$tracks, function(track, id) {     data.frame(       id   = id,       time = track$timestamp,       x    = track$px,       y    = track$py,       stringsAsFactors = FALSE     )   })   do.call(rbind, entries) } register_loader_dialect("json_tracks", json_tracks_fn) ts_json <- TrajSet_read(json_tracks_fn(fake_json)) ts_json #> TrajSet: 2 trajectories, 6 observations #> Columns: id='id', time='time', angle='angle' (radians), x='x', y='y', raw_x='x_raw', raw_y='y_raw' #>     id time         x          y x_raw y_raw rho     angle #> A.1  A    0 0.0000000  0.0000000   0.0   0.0  NA 0.0000000 #> A.2  A    1 0.9805807  0.1961161   1.0   0.2   1 0.1973956 #> A.3  A    2 0.9701425  0.2425356   2.0   0.5   1 0.2449787 #> B.1  B    0 1.0000000  0.0000000   1.0   0.0   1 0.0000000 #> B.2  B    1 0.9970545 -0.0766965   1.3  -0.1   1 6.2064134 #> B.3  B    2 0.9912279 -0.1321637   1.5  -0.2   1 6.1506338

title: radiatR
text: radiatR streamlines journey raw tracking files publication-ready plots experiments conducted circular arenas. package includes: utilities importing paired landmark/track text files produced popular tracking suites; helpers extracting trial limits computing circular summary statistics; polished ggplot2 layers drawing concentric guides annotated paths.

title: radiatR
text: package ships five arc = 0° example tracks demonstrate full import workflow. Landmark files (_point01.txt) record two pixel-space reference points per trial (arena centre reference point arena wall); track files (_point02.txt) contain full xy trajectory. get_all_object_pos() reads landmark/track pair, normalises coordinates unit circle (arena radius = 1), returns TrajSet. Trial metadata (arena radius, reference position, frame limits) ts_demo@meta$trial_limits.
code: track_dir     <- system.file("extdata", "tracks", package = "radiatR") manifest_path <- system.file("extdata", "P_lividus_trials.csv", package = "radiatR")  file_tbl  <- import_tracks(track_dir) manifest  <- import_info(manifest_path) file_tbl  <- load_tracks(file_tbl, manifest, track_dir)  ts_demo <- get_all_object_pos(file_tbl = file_tbl, track_dir = track_dir) ts_demo #> TrajSet: 5 trajectories, 2049 observations #> Columns: id='trial_id', time='frame', angle='rel_theta' (radians), x='trans_x', y='trans_y' #> Transform steps: unit_circle_mapping  #> # A tibble: 6 × 15 #>   trial_id       frame     x     y trans_x trans_y trans_rho abs_theta rel_theta #>   <chr>          <int> <dbl> <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1 G1D_0_obstacl…     1 1045.  559.   0.181 -0.0395     0.186      6.07      3.93 #> 2 G1D_0_obstacl…     2 1045.  559.   0.181 -0.0395     0.186      6.07      3.93 #> 3 G1D_0_obstacl…     3 1050.  560.   0.191 -0.0407     0.196      6.07      3.93 #> 4 G1D_0_obstacl…     4 1050.  560.   0.191 -0.0407     0.196      6.07      3.93 #> 5 G1D_0_obstacl…     5 1052.  560.   0.197 -0.0407     0.202      6.08      3.94 #> 6 G1D_0_obstacl…     6 1053.  560.   0.199 -0.0407     0.203      6.08      3.94 #> # ℹ 6 more variables: rel_x <dbl>, rel_y <dbl>, video <chr>, order <chr>, #> #   vid_ord <chr>, radius <dbl>

title: radiatR
text: crossing method — projecting vector two concentric-ring crossings unit circle — assigns one directional heading per trial. derive_headings() return_coords = TRUE returns heading angle inner-ring crossing position. Overlaying heading endpoints (one hollow circle per trial) grand mean direction combined trajectory plot gives compact summary full dataset:  grand mean arrow points 320.9° relative reference direction (clock convention; 0° = toward reference) R = 0.08, reflecting overall reference-relative tendency across conditions.
code: hd <- derive_headings(plividus, rule = "crossing",                       circ0 = 0.2, circ1 = 0.4,                       coords = "relative",                       angle_convention = "clock",                       return_coords = TRUE) # add arc grouping for faceting hd$arc <- factor(   as.integer(sub("G1D_(\\d+)_.*", "\\1", hd$id)),   levels = c(0, 15, 30, 45, 60, 150),   labels = c("0°", "15°", "30°", "45°", "60°", "150°") ) names(hd)[names(hd) == "id"] <- "trial_id" attr(hd, "colour_col") <- "arc" head(hd[, c("trial_id", "arc", "heading", "x_inner", "y_inner")]) #>                                     trial_id arc   heading     x_inner #> 1 G1D_0_obstacle/WIN_20210201_11_24_19_Pro_1  0° 2.4162200 -0.14005857 #> 2 G1D_0_obstacle/WIN_20210201_11_32_56_Pro_1  0° 2.6851092 -0.09694777 #> 3 G1D_0_obstacle/WIN_20210204_16_55_13_Pro_1  0° 2.0733246  0.07213251 #> 4 G1D_0_obstacle/WIN_20210204_17_12_38_Pro_1  0° 2.0834057 -0.18174038 #> 5 G1D_0_obstacle/WIN_20210204_17_30_19_Pro_1  0° 0.3838676  0.19833263 #> 6 G1D_0_obstacle/WIN_20210204_17_44_09_Pro_1  0° 2.6079536 -0.19480474 #>       y_inner #> 1 -0.14277011 #> 2 -0.17493178 #> 3 -0.18652985 #> 4 -0.08347969 #> 5 -0.02577011 #> 6 -0.04528552 p_all <- radiate(plividus,                  group_col   = "trial_id",                  colour_col  = "arc",                  show_labels = FALSE,                  show_arrow  = FALSE) +   add_heading_points(hd, colour_col = "arc", size = 1, alpha = 0.6)  p_all + add_heading_arrow(hd) #> Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`).

title: radiatR
text: Three functions handle directional uncertainty arcs parallel density overlay functions: Two statistics supported: stat = "bootstrap_ci" bootstraps von Mises MLE confidence interval mean direction; stat = "sd" draws ±1 circular SD arc. split design lets substitute Bayesian credible bounds data frame rendering:
code: iv <- compute_circ_interval(hd, colour_col = "arc", stat = "bootstrap_ci") # replace with Bayesian posteriors: iv$lower <- ...; iv$upper <- ... add_circ_interval(iv, colour_col = "arc")

title: radiatR
text: Layers compose standard ggplot2 + operator, plot can assembled feature feature. four chunks start trajectories add heading endpoints, grand mean arrow, finally bootstrap CI arc. Trajectories:  + Heading endpoints trial’s crossing location:  + Grand mean direction arrow:  arc radius 1.05 spans 95 % bootstrap confidence interval grand mean direction pooled across arc conditions.
code: p <- radiate(plividus,              group_col   = "trial_id",              colour_col  = "arc",              show_labels = FALSE,              show_arrow  = FALSE) p p <- p + add_heading_points(hd, colour_col = "arc", size = 1.5, alpha = 0.7) p #> Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`). p <- p + add_heading_arrow(hd) p #> Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`).

title: radiatR
text: Three strategies control trajectories coloured. Option 1 — single colour. Pass colour string directly heading-overlay helpers; omit colour_col radiate() draw tracks default grey. Option 2 — cycling palette. colour_cycle assigns trajectory colour index cycles back 1 every n trajectories. panel_by set cycle restarts independently within panel trajectory 1 every panel always gets colour 1. Pass integer automatic palette assignment, character vector specify colours explicitly. Option 3 — variable mapping. colour_col maps data column colour (e.g. arc factor used combined-trajectory plot ).

title: radiatR
text: Faceting arc angle shows condition separately. Within panel, assign_cycle_colours() distinguishes individual trajectories cycling 10 colours, resetting panel boundary. Calling explicitly track data headings data frame (joining trial id) means heading markers inherit exact per-trajectory colour — single per-condition colour — colour_col used .  + Bootstrap CI arc added first sits behind heading markers:  + Heading vectors (dotted lines inner crossing perimeter):  + Heading points top CI arc:   panel shows trajectories heading markers coloured per-trajectory cycling palette, bootstrap CI arc radius 1.05 condition colour (rendered behind heading points), solid mean direction arrow, degree labels. clock convention (0° = toward reference, clockwise). Use strip_position = "inside" place label inside plot area, strip_labels = FALSE suppress .
code: # Pre-compute cycling colours: 10 colours, restarting within each arc panel. # Join to headings so heading layers can use the same column. plividus_cc        <- plividus plividus_cc@data   <- assign_cycle_colours(plividus@data,                                             id_col    = "trial_id",                                             n         = 10,                                             panel_col = "arc") colour_map <- unique(plividus_cc@data[, c("trial_id", "cycle_colour")]) hd_cc      <- merge(hd, colour_map,                     by      = "trial_id", all.x = TRUE) # merge() strips attributes — restore convention metadata from hd attr(hd_cc, "angle_convention")   <- attr(hd, "angle_convention") attr(hd_cc, "coords")             <- attr(hd, "coords") attr(hd_cc, "display_convention") <- attr(hd, "display_convention") attr(hd_cc, "colour_col")         <- "cycle_colour" p <- radiate(plividus_cc,         group_col    = "trial_id",         colour_col   = "cycle_colour",         panel_by     = "arc",         ncol         = 3,         show_labels  = FALSE,         show_arrow   = FALSE) p p <- p + add_heading_interval(hd_cc, colour_col = "arc", colour = "black",                                stat = "bootstrap_ci", boot_reps = 999L) p p <- p + add_heading_vectors(hd_cc) p #> Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_segment()`). p <- p + add_heading_points(hd_cc, size = 4) p #> Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_segment()`). #> Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`). p <- p + add_heading_arrow(hd_cc, colour_col = "arc", colour = "black") p #> Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_segment()`). #> Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`).

title: radiatR
text: Three functions handle directional density overlays; designed compose cleanly density source: Three built-methods available compute_circular_density(): "vonmises" (MLE via circular::mle.vonmises()), "kernel" (circular KDE), "histogram" (bin counts). computation rendering steps separate, density column can replaced values model — including Bayesian posterior predictive density brms — calling add_circular_density(): convenience wrapper add_heading_density() skips intermediate step substitution needed. combined panel plot uses shade per-condition von Mises density alongside heading vectors:  shaded region von Mises density fitted crossing headings within arc condition. narrower, taller peak indicates concentrated directional responses. Switch method = "kernel" non-parametric estimate, method = "histogram" raw count display.
code: dens_df <- compute_circular_density(hd, colour_col = "arc", method = "vonmises") # replace with Bayesian posterior: dens_df$density <- my_brms_fitted_density(dens_df$theta) add_circular_density(dens_df, colour_col = "arc", fill = "grey80", alpha = 0.35) radiate(plividus_cc,         group_col    = "trial_id",         colour_col   = "cycle_colour",         panel_by     = "arc",         ncol         = 3,         show_labels  = FALSE,         show_arrow   = FALSE) +   add_heading_density(hd_cc, colour_col = "arc",                       method = "vonmises", scale = 0.4,                       fill = "grey80", alpha = 0.35) +   add_heading_vectors(hd_cc) +   add_heading_arrow(hd_cc, colour_col = "arc", colour = "black") #> Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_segment()`).

title: radiatR
text: compute_circular_density() boot_reps > 0 runs non-parametric bootstrap: heading samples drawn replacement, von Mises MLE fitted replicate, density re-evaluated angular grid. boot_alpha / 2 1 - boot_alpha / 2 quantiles across replicates become density_lower density_upper columns, add_circular_density() renders shaded band around fitted curve.  darker band 95% bootstrap confidence interval fitted von Mises density. wider band reflects greater parametric uncertainty, typically seen smaller diffuse conditions. density_lower density_upper columns dens_boot can replaced interval values Bayesian model (e.g. 2.5th 97.5th percentiles brms posterior predictive distribution) passing add_circular_density().
code: dens_boot <- compute_circular_density(hd, colour_col = "arc",                                       method = "vonmises",                                       boot_reps = 499L, boot_alpha = 0.05,                                       n_theta = 300L)  radiate(plividus,         group_col    = "trial_id",         colour_cycle = 10,         panel_by     = "arc",         ncol         = 3,         show_labels  = FALSE,         show_arrow   = FALSE) +   add_circular_density(dens_boot, colour_col = "arc",                        scale = 0.4, fill = "grey80", alpha = 0.35,                        ci_fill = "grey60", ci_alpha = 0.35) +   add_heading_arrow(hd, colour_col = "arc", colour = "black")

title: radiatR
text: compute_circ_mean() returns per-condition statistics behind arrows : within-trial path consistency (tortuosity), circ_summary() operates step--step angle distribution: High resultant lengths (close 1) indicate consistent step directions within trial.

title: radiatR
text: derive_headings() supports fourteen built-rules. "crossing" well suited echinoderm-style tracks — moderate tortuosity, consistent outward movement — rules may appropriate depending taxon experimental design. Use list_heading_rules() see available names; custom rules can added register_heading_rule(). Two parameter-free alternatives especially useful:

title: radiatR
text: distal rule takes atan2(y, x) frame animal farthest arena centre. requires ring parameters never returns NA every trial -distal frame. Per-condition resultant lengths "distal" close "crossing", consistent P. lividus tracks fairly direct: Visualising distal headings per-condition panel confirms spatial pattern consistent crossing method:  P. lividus tracks nearly radial, heading estimates agree closely across methods. sinuous species (moths, pigeons) choice rule larger influence result.

return_coords = TRUE) hd_distal$arc <- factor(   as.integer(sub("G1D_(\\d+)_.*", "\\1", hd_distal$id)),   levels = c(0, 15, 30, 45, 60, 150),   labels = c("0°", "15°", "30°", "45°", "60°", "150°") ) names(hd_distal)[names(hd_distal) == "id"] <- "trial_id" sm_cross  <- compute_circ_mean(hd,       colour_col = "arc")[, c("arc", "resultant_R")] sm_distal <- compute_circ_mean(hd_distal, colour_col = "arc")[, c("arc", "resultant_R")] names(sm_cross)[2]  <- "crossing" names(sm_distal)[2] <- "distal" merge(sm_cross, sm_distal, by = "arc") #>    arc   crossing     distal #> 1   0° 0.08994719 0.09866019 #> 2  15° 0.13925405 0.12900871 #> 3 150° 0.13477135 0.14318298 #> 4  30° 0.15168934 0.15934680 #> 5  45° 0.17151292 0.18744397 #> 6  60° 0.23689999 0.19465067 plividus_cc_d        <- "trial_id", all.x = TRUE) attr(hd_distal_cc, "angle_convention")   <- attr(hd_distal, "angle_convention") attr(hd_distal_cc, "coords")             <- attr(hd_distal, "coords") attr(hd_distal_cc, title: radiatR
text: net rule returns direction vector first recorded position last, ignoring intermediate points. fastest possible estimate natural match GPS vanishing-bearing studies release final observed positions available. tracks three methods give similar resultant lengths. Larger differences expected species complex search behaviour committing direction.
code: hd_net <- derive_headings(plividus, rule = "net",                            coords = "relative",                            angle_convention = "clock") hd_net$arc <- factor(   as.integer(sub("G1D_(\\d+)_.*", "\\1", hd_net$id)),   levels = c(0, 15, 30, 45, 60, 150),   labels = c("0°", "15°", "30°", "45°", "60°", "150°") ) names(hd_net)[names(hd_net) == "id"] <- "trial_id"  sm_net <- compute_circ_mean(hd_net, colour_col = "arc")[, c("arc", "resultant_R")] names(sm_net)[2] <- "net" Reduce(function(a, b) merge(a, b, by = "arc"),        list(sm_cross, sm_distal, sm_net)) #>    arc   crossing     distal       net #> 1   0° 0.08994719 0.09866019 0.1003765 #> 2  15° 0.13925405 0.12900871 0.1350682 #> 3 150° 0.13477135 0.14318298 0.1382390 #> 4  30° 0.15168934 0.15934680 0.1635828 #> 5  45° 0.17151292 0.18744397 0.1858180 #> 6  60° 0.23689999 0.19465067 0.1978115

title: radiatR
text: simulate_tracks() generates synthetic trajectories without files, useful testing demonstrations. kappa parameter controls directedness:  left panel (low kappa) shows tortuous paths; right (high kappa) shows straighter, directed tracks. mean resultant arrow computed independently per panel.
code: sim_df <- simulate_tracks(   conditions = data.frame(n_trials = c(5L, 5L), kappa = c(2, 8)),   n_points = 150,   seed = 42 ) ts_sim <- TrajSet(   sim_df,   id = "trial_id", time = "frame",   angle = "rel_theta", x = "rel_x", y = "rel_y",   angle_unit = "radians", normalize_xy = FALSE ) radiate(ts_sim,         group_col    = "trial_id",         colour_cycle = 5,         panel_by     = "condition",         ncol         = 2,         show_arrow   = TRUE)

title: Simulating Trajectories
text: simulate_tracks() generates synthetic circular-arena trajectories without tracking files. main uses : Verifying new analysis pipeline works real data arrives. Reproducing plots examples documentation. Teaching: demonstrating concentration, tortuosity, directional bias affect appearance paths. Informal power analysis: generate data plausible effect size check whether expected statistical pattern visible.

title: Simulating Trajectories
text: Called arguments, simulate_tracks() returns tibble three conditions (60 trials total, 200 frames ): row one frame. key columns :
code: sim <- simulate_tracks(seed = 1) dim(sim) #> [1] 12000    16 names(sim) #>  [1] "condition"     "trial_id"      "trial_num"     "frame"         #>  [5] "predictor"     "concentration" "tortuosity"    "ref_heading"   #>  [9] "final_heading" "rho"           "abs_theta"     "rel_theta"     #> [13] "abs_x"         "abs_y"         "rel_x"         "rel_y"

title: Simulating Trajectories
text: output argument controls returned. output = "trajset" path wraps tibble TrajSet (absolute coordinates, normalisation). Use whenever want plug straight plotting analysis functions.
code: # Default: long-form tibble sim_tbl  <- simulate_tracks(seed = 1, output = "tibble")  # TrajSet directly (ready for radiate(), derive_headings(), etc.) sim_ts   <- simulate_tracks(seed = 1, output = "trajset")  # Both representations in a named list sim_both <- simulate_tracks(seed = 1, output = "both") names(sim_both)  # "tibble" "trajset"

title: Simulating Trajectories
text: three default conditions differ directional bias, concentration, tortuosity (see ).
code: ts_default <- simulate_tracks(seed = 1, output = "trajset") radiate(ts_default,         group_col    = "trial_id",         colour_cycle = 10,         panel_by     = "condition",         ncol         = 3,         show_labels  = FALSE,         show_arrow   = TRUE)

title: Simulating Trajectories
text: simulation behaviour controlled data frame passed conditions. conditions = NULL function fills three-condition template. Supply data frame override subset columns; missing columns receive sensible defaults.

title: Simulating Trajectories
text: concentration_base von Mises κ passed rvonmises(). Higher values produce tightly clustered headings; values near zero produce near-uniform heading distributions.  κ = 1 mean arrow short (low resultant length); κ = 12 nearly trials point direction.
code: conds_kappa <- data.frame(   condition          = c("low (κ=0.1)", "medium (κ=1)", "high (κ=12)"),   n_trials           = 20L,   concentration_base = c(0.1, 1, 12),   tortuosity_base    = 0.05 ) ts_kappa <- simulate_tracks(conditions = conds_kappa, seed = 42,                              output = "trajset") radiate(ts_kappa,         group_col    = "trial_id",         colour_cycle = 10,         panel_by     = "condition",         ncol         = 3,         show_labels  = FALSE,         show_arrow   = TRUE)

title: Simulating Trajectories
text: Two parameters control within-trial path shape: tortuosity_base — SD per-step angular noise. Larger values produce sinuous paths. phi — autocorrelation successive angular deviations (0 = white noise, values near 1 produce smooth, sweeping curves). Passed directly simulate_tracks(), via conditions table.
code: conds_tort <- data.frame(   condition          = c("smooth (φ=0.95)", "moderate (φ=0.7)", "noisy (φ=0)"),   n_trials           = 15L,   concentration_base = 6,   tortuosity_base    = c(0.12, 0.12, 0.12) ) phi_vals <- c(0.95, 0.7, 0)  # Simulate each phi separately and bind parts <- lapply(seq_along(phi_vals), function(i) {   d <- conds_tort[i, , drop = FALSE]   simulate_tracks(conditions = d, n_points = 200,                   phi = phi_vals[i], seed = 7 + i) }) ts_tort <- TrajSet(   do.call(rbind, parts),   id = "trial_id", time = "frame",   x = "abs_x", y = "abs_y", angle = "abs_theta",   angle_unit = "radians", normalize_xy = FALSE ) radiate(ts_tort,         group_col    = "trial_id",         colour_cycle = 10,         panel_by     = "condition",         ncol         = 3,         show_labels  = FALSE,         show_arrow   = FALSE)

title: Simulating Trajectories
text: ref_mean shifts reference direction away East (0 rad). Use simulate experiments reference along positive x-axis.
code: conds_bias <- data.frame(   condition          = c("East (0°)", "North (90°)", "Northwest (135°)"),   n_trials           = 20L,   concentration_base = 5,   tortuosity_base    = 0.06,   ref_mean          = c(0, pi / 2, 3 * pi / 4) ) ts_bias <- simulate_tracks(conditions = conds_bias, seed = 99,                            output = "trajset") radiate(ts_bias,         group_col    = "trial_id",         colour_cycle = 10,         panel_by     = "condition",         ncol         = 3,         show_labels  = FALSE,         show_arrow   = TRUE)

title: Simulating Trajectories
text: common design monotone gradient cue strength. example simulates four levels increasing concentration decreasing tortuosity — mimicking orientation experiment stronger cues produce tighter, smoother paths.
code: conds_grad <- data.frame(   condition          = paste0(c(15, 30, 60, 120), "° arc"),   n_trials           = 25L,   concentration_base = c(1.5, 3, 6, 10),   tortuosity_base    = c(0.14, 0.10, 0.06, 0.03),   tortuosity_sd      = 0.015 ) ts_grad <- simulate_tracks(conditions = conds_grad, n_points = 200,                             seed = 55, output = "trajset") radiate(ts_grad,         group_col    = "trial_id",         colour_col   = "condition",         panel_by     = "condition",         ncol         = 4,         show_labels  = FALSE,         show_arrow   = TRUE)

title: Simulating Trajectories
text: concentration_slope tortuosity_slope non-zero, final kappa tortuosity trial depend sampled predictor value. lets model experiments individual differences (e.g. body size, prior exposure) modulate response. Plotting concentration predictor confirms linear relationship:
code: conds_pred <- data.frame(   condition            = "gradient",   n_trials             = 40L,   concentration_base   = 3,   concentration_slope  = 4,    # kappa rises with predictor   tortuosity_base      = 0.10,   tortuosity_slope     = -0.04, # smoother at high predictor   predictor_mean       = 0,   predictor_sd         = 1 ) sim_pred <- simulate_tracks(conditions = conds_pred, seed = 7, output = "both")  # Each trial gets its own predictor sample range(sim_pred$tibble$predictor) #> [1] -1.387996  2.716752 range(sim_pred$tibble$concentration) #> [1]  0.10000 13.86701 trial_summary <- unique(sim_pred$tibble[, c("trial_id", "predictor",                                              "concentration")]) ggplot(trial_summary, aes(predictor, concentration)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +   geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, colour = "steelblue") +   labs(x = "Predictor", y = "Effective κ") +   theme_minimal() #> `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

title: Simulating Trajectories
text: Simulated TrajSet objects feed directly derive_headings() circ_summarise(), making straightforward run analysis pipeline synthetic data. Visualise headings alongside trajectories:
code: conds_analysis <- data.frame(   condition          = c("diffuse", "concentrated"),   n_trials           = 30L,   concentration_base = c(1.5, 8),   tortuosity_base    = c(0.12, 0.04) ) sim_analysis <- simulate_tracks(conditions = conds_analysis, seed = 21,                                  output = "both")  hd <- derive_headings(sim_analysis$trajset, rule = "crossing",                        circ0 = 0.2, circ1 = 0.4,                        coords = "absolute",                        angle_convention = "unit_circle",                        return_coords = TRUE) names(hd)[names(hd) == "id"] <- "trial_id"  # derive_headings returns only id/heading/coords; join condition back cond_map <- unique(sim_analysis$tibble[, c("trial_id", "condition")]) hd <- merge(hd, cond_map, by = "trial_id")  compute_circ_mean(hd, colour_col = "condition")[,   c("condition", "mean_dir", "resultant_R")] #> `angle_convention` not found in headings_df attributes; defaulting to 'unit_circle'. #> `coords` not found in headings_df attributes; defaulting to 'absolute'. #>      condition   mean_dir resultant_R #> 1 concentrated 0.05580664   0.9028539 #> 2      diffuse 0.29537110   0.7004167 p <- radiate(sim_analysis$trajset,              group_col    = "trial_id",              colour_col   = "condition",              panel_by     = "condition",              ncol         = 2,              show_labels  = FALSE,              show_arrow   = FALSE) +   add_heading_points(hd, colour_col = "condition", size = 2, alpha = 0.7) +   add_heading_arrow(hd, colour_col = "condition", colour = "black") #> `angle_convention` not found in headings_df attributes; defaulting to 'unit_circle'. #> `coords` not found in headings_df attributes; defaulting to 'absolute'. p

Derive headings hd <- derive_headings(ts, rule = "crossing",                       circ0 = 0.3, circ1 = 0.6,                       coords = "absolute",                       angle_convention = "unit_circle")  # 3. title: Add a critical resultant-length circle to a radiate plot — add_critical_r
text: Two tests supported: "rayleigh" (default) Tests uniformity     hypothesised direction.  Critical value     \(\bar R_{crit} = \sqrt{-\log(\alpha) / n}\) (asymptotic; accurate     \(n \ge 10\)). "vtest" Tests specific direction.  Critical value     \(\bar R_{crit} = z_\alpha / \sqrt{2n}\) powerful (    observed mean direction equals hypothesised \(\mu_0\));     effective threshold rises observed direction departs     \(\mu_0\), circle lower bound. Sample size n taken per group hd.  group_col matches parent radiate(panel_by = ...) argument, one circle drawn per panel radius appropriate panel's n.  groups overlaid single panel, set per_group = TRUE draw one circle per group (colour-matched), per_group = FALSE (default) draw single conservative circle using smallest n (largest critical radius).

text: Tidy data frame columns group_col (supplied),   r, n, type, test = TRUE also   statistic, df, p_value.

title: Circular correlation between headings and a covariate — circ_cor
text: Circular-linear (T-linear association, Mardia \& Jupp 2000): $$r^2 = \frac{r_{cx}^2 + r_{cy}^2 - 2r_{cx}r_{cy}r_{xy}}{1 - r_{xy}^2}$$ \(r_{cx} = \text{cor}(\cos\theta, x)\), \(r_{cy} = \text{cor}(\sin\theta, x)\), \(r_{xy} = \text{cor}(\cos\theta, \sin\theta)\). \(r \[0, 1]\); test statistic \(n r^2 \sim \chi^2_2\) \(H_0\).  Note: \(r\) unsigned (association strength , direction). Circular-circular (Fisher's \(\rho\), via cor.circular): \(r \[-1, 1]\).

title: Compute a circular density data frame from heading observations — compute_circular_density
text: Three built-estimation methods provided: * `"vonmises"` – fit von Mises distribution MLE   ([circular::mle.vonmises()]) evaluate fitted density regular   grid `n_theta` angles. Bootstrap confidence bands available via   `boot_reps`. * `"kernel"` – circular kernel density estimate   ([circular::density.circular()]) bandwidth chosen   [circular::bw.nrd.circular()] unless `bw` supplied. * `"histogram"` – angular bin counts (circular rose diagram); `bins`   controls number bins. `colour_col` supplied density computed independently group group label preserved output, enabling per-panel use [radiate()]'s `panel_by`. `boot_reps > 0` `method = "vonmises"`, non-parametric bootstrap run: `boot_reps` samples drawn replacement, von Mises MLE fitted , density evaluated grid. `boot_alpha / 2` `1 - boot_alpha / 2` quantiles across replicates returned `density_lower` `density_upper` columns. can rendered confidence band [add_circular_density()], replaced interval values Bayesian model plotting.

what: radiatR: Analysis and Visualisation of Circular Arena Tracking Data — radiatR-package
title: radiatR: Analysis and Visualisation of Circular Arena Tracking Data — radiatR-package
text: complete pipeline analysing animal orientation movement circular arenas. Reads data 20+ tracking tools (EthoVision, DeepLabCut, SLEAP, TRex, -maze, TrackMate, idtracker.ai, Ctrax, others) via unified dialect-based loader system, including multi-bodypart pose estimation likelihood-weighted centroid. Derives per-trial heading directions using multiple rules (ring-crossing, distal point, pose body-axis, velocity-based, ). Computes circular statistics including mean direction, resultant length, von Mises wrapped Cauchy parametric fits, circular correlation (circular-linear circular-circular), multi-sample hypothesis tests (Watson-Williams, Rayleigh, others) multiple-comparison correction. Renders publication-quality ggplot2 track plots overlaid rose diagrams, parametric density curves, non-parametric kernel density estimates. Includes browser-based graphical interface requiring R coding via launch_app().

title: Simulate trajectory sets under configurable experimental conditions — simulate_tracks
text: n_points Integer number frames per trajectory. conditions Optional data frame describing experimental conditions. See Details column descriptions. omitted, three-condition template used. output Character string controlling return type: "tibble" (default) returns long-form data frame, "trajset" returns [TrajSet] object, "" returns list containing representations. write_path Optional file path (CSV) simulated data written. seed Optional integer seed supplied [set.seed()] reproducibility. radial_noise Standard deviation radial noise applied unit radius profile. phi Autocorrelation parameter (0-1) used generating angular noise series; higher values produce smoother paths.

title: Simulate trajectory sets under configurable experimental conditions — simulate_tracks
text: Depending `output`, tibble, `TrajSet`, list containing   . `write_path` supplied data also written disk.

title: Simulate trajectory sets under configurable experimental conditions — simulate_tracks
text: `conditions` data frame can contain following columns (defaults supplied missing): - `condition` (character): condition label. - `n_trials` (integer): number trajectories simulate condition. - `ref_mean` (numeric radian): baseline reference heading (default 0). - `concentration_base` (numeric): baseline von Mises concentration (kappa). - `concentration_slope` (numeric): optional slope applied predictor. - `tortuosity_base` (numeric): baseline angular noise scale. - `tortuosity_slope` (numeric): slope applied predictor. - `tortuosity_sd` (numeric): additional random variation per trial. - `predictor_mean`, `predictor_sd` (numeric): parameters used sample   per-trial predictor explicit values supplied. - `predictor_values` (list-column): optional explicit predictor values (length   `n_trials`) overriding generated values. predictor can represent continuous covariate (e.g. reference intensity). final heading concentration increases larger kappa, whereas larger tortuosity values produce sinuous paths.

title: Simulate trajectory sets under configurable experimental conditions — simulate_tracks
code: sim <- simulate_tracks(seed = 1) head(sim) #> # A tibble: 6 × 16 #>   condition trial_id    trial_num frame predictor concentration tortuosity #>   <chr>     <chr>           <int> <int>     <dbl>         <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1 control   control_001         1     1    -0.125          7.87     0.0407 #> 2 control   control_001         1     2    -0.125          7.87     0.0407 #> 3 control   control_001         1     3    -0.125          7.87     0.0407 #> 4 control   control_001         1     4    -0.125          7.87     0.0407 #> 5 control   control_001         1     5    -0.125          7.87     0.0407 #> 6 control   control_001         1     6    -0.125          7.87     0.0407 #> # ℹ 9 more variables: ref_heading <dbl>, final_heading <dbl>, rho <dbl>, #> #   abs_theta <dbl>, rel_theta <dbl>, abs_x <dbl>, abs_y <dbl>, rel_x <dbl>, #> #   rel_y <dbl>  # Request both tibble and TrajSet representations sim_both <- simulate_tracks(output = "both", seed = 123) names(sim_both) #> [1] "tibble"  "trajset"  example_conditions <- tibble::tibble(   condition = paste0("level_", 1:4),   n_trials = 30L,   ref_mean = seq(-pi/8, pi/8, length.out = 4),   concentration_base = c(2, 4, 6, 8),   concentration_slope = 0.8,   tortuosity_base = c(0.12, 0.09, 0.06, 0.04),   tortuosity_slope = -0.01,   tortuosity_sd = 0.015,   predictor_mean = seq(-1, 1, length.out = 4),   predictor_sd = 0.15 ) sim_four_levels <- simulate_tracks(   n_points = 120,   conditions = example_conditions,   output = "both",   seed = 42 ) aggregate(trial_id ~ condition, data = sim_four_levels$tibble, length) #>   condition trial_id #> 1   level_1     3600 #> 2   level_2     3600 #> 3   level_3     3600 #> 4   level_4     3600 names(sim_four_levels) #> [1] "tibble"  "trajset"
